results.possibleIdentity: Norman Nelson (likely Norman F. Nelson) was an engineer and technical professional associated with the Union Camp Corporation during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Based in Wayne, New Jersey, where Union Camp was headquartered, Nelson specialized in paper technology and structural materials, specifically honeycomb structural papers. He is notably recognized for his collaboration with the non-profit organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which fostered partnerships between the arts and industrial engineering. On February 27, 1968, Nelson delivered a lecture-demonstration titled "Honeycomb Structural Papers" at the E.A.T. loft in New York City as part of a series intended to introduce artists to new industrial materials and processes. He is likely the same Norman F. Nelson who graduated from the SUNY College of Environmental Science and Forestry (ESF) in 1957, an institution closely linked to the paper and wood products industry.
